# Cookie Policy

_UK GDPR required document template. Replace all [placeholders] with your organisation details before publication._

**Source:** `External - Customer-Facing Documents/Cookie Policy.docx`

---

Cookie Policy

## Uk Gdpr

Introduction

About this policy

This Cookie Policy explains how [Company Name] ("[Company Name]", "we", "us") uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit our websites, access the [Product Name] web application, or otherwise interact with our online services.

It applies to users in the United Kingdom, including England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland.

Please read this policy together with our [Product Name] Privacy Policy and Terms of Service.

Last updated: May 2026. We may update this policy; material changes will be signposted on our website or in the [Product Name] application.

Who is responsible

[Company Name] is the data controller for information collected through cookies and similar technologies on our own websites and marketing pages, except where your organisation is the controller for workforce analytics configured by your administrator.

For questions: privacy@[company-domain].

Supervisory authority: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) — ico.org.uk.

Legal framework

### Our use of cookies is regulated by

Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations (PECR) 2003 — Regulation 6 (consent for storing/accessing information on terminal equipment)

UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) — where cookie data identifies individuals

Data Protection Act 2018

We obtain valid consent before placing non-essential cookies, except for strictly necessary cookies as described below.

What Are Cookies and Similar Technologies

Definitions

Cookies are small text files placed on your computer, tablet, or mobile device when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work, improve efficiency, and provide information to site owners.

### Similar technologies include

Local storage and session storage — browser storage used by [Product Name] for preferences and session state

Pixels / web beacons — small graphics that may be used in emails or pages to understand engagement

SDKs or device identifiers — where used in mobile or embedded clients (post-MVP)

Throughout this policy, "cookies" includes these technologies unless we state otherwise.

First and third party

First-party cookies are set by [Company Name] (or the [Product Name] domain)

Third-party cookies are set by another domain (e.g. analytics or authentication providers)

We minimise third-party cookies and document them in the tables below.

Session and persistent

Session cookies expire when you close your browser

Persistent cookies remain until they expire or you delete them

Retention periods for each cookie are listed in the cookie tables below.

How We Use Cookies on [Product Name]

Purposes

### We use cookies and similar technologies on [Product Name] to

Enable you to log in securely and maintain your session

Protect the service from fraud, abuse, and security threats

Remember your preferences (where you have consented or where strictly necessary)

Understand aggregated usage to improve reliability and usability

Comply with legal obligations

We do not use cookies to read the content of your notes or work items for advertising purposes. Analytics, where enabled, uses aggregated metadata only.

UK-only service

[Product Name] is offered in the United Kingdom only. Cookie data is processed in connection with services hosted in Microsoft Azure UK South. See our Privacy Policy and Subprocessor List for details.

Categories of Cookies

Overview

We group cookies into the categories below. Non-essential categories require your consent under PECR before they are placed, except where an exemption applies.

Marketing

We do not deploy advertising or cross-site tracking cookies in the [Product Name] application at launch. If this changes, we will update this register and request consent before any marketing cookies are placed.

Updates to register

We review this register when we add features, subprocessors, or integrations. Subprocessor cookies are limited to what is necessary for authentication, hosting, and consented analytics.

Consent and Your Choices

Cookie banner

When you first visit our website or [Product Name] login/marketing pages, you will see a cookie banner allowing you to:

Accept all cookies

Reject non-essential cookies

Customise preferences by category

Your choice is stored in [product]_consent (or equivalent) so we do not repeatedly ask unless you clear storage or we materially change cookies.

Changing your mind

### You may withdraw or change consent at any time by

Using "Cookie settings" in the website footer or [Product Name] account settings (where available)

Clearing cookies in your browser and revisiting the banner

Contacting us at privacy@[company-domain]

Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before withdrawal.

Browser controls

Most browsers let you block or delete cookies. Instructions are available from your browser provider (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge, etc.).

If you block strictly necessary cookies, parts of [Product Name] may not function (including login).

For mobile devices, refer to OS settings for advertising identifiers where applicable.

Do Not Track

There is no uniform industry standard for "Do Not Track" signals. We honour your cookie consent choices via our banner and settings rather than DNT headers alone.

Third Parties and Authentication

Microsoft Azure AD B2C

[Product Name] uses Azure AD B2C for authentication. Microsoft may set cookies necessary to perform login, MFA, and password reset. These are strictly necessary for access to [Product Name].

Microsoft's privacy statement: https://privacy.microsoft.com

Other subprocessors

Our Subprocessor List describes providers that may process personal data. Only providers that set cookies in your browser are listed in the cookie register above.

We require subprocessors to comply with UK data protection law and contract terms no less protective than our DPA.

Personal Data and UK GDPR

Relationship to Privacy Policy

Where cookie data identifies you (e.g. combined with account ID), it is personal data under UK GDPR.

Lawful bases may include consent (non-essential cookies), legitimate interests (security logs where applicable), and contract (strictly necessary cookies to deliver [Product Name]).

See the [Product Name] Privacy Policy for full details on retention, rights, and international transfers.

Your rights

You have rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection under UK GDPR, and the right to lodge a complaint with the ICO.

To exercise rights: privacy@[company-domain]. We respond within one month unless an extension applies.

Children

Age

[Product Name] is a business productivity service not directed at children under 18. We do not knowingly place non-essential cookies for child-directed services.

If you believe we have collected data from a child, contact privacy@[company-domain].

Changes and Contact

Changes

We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect legal, technical, or product changes. We will post the new version with an updated date. For material changes affecting non-essential cookies, we will seek fresh consent where required.

Contact

Email: privacy@[company-domain]

Website: https://www.[company-domain]

[Product Name] application: https://app.[company-domain]
